name: George Will
summary: Pulitzer Prize-winning conservative columnist who left the Republican Party over Trump and became one of his most intellectually rigorous critics, regularly writing columns condemning Trump's character and policies.

keyStatements.quote: Trump has an unerring instinct for the lowest common denominator. His is a coarse and corrupting presidency... This unraveling presidency began with the Crybaby-in-Chief banging his spoon on his highchair tray to protest a photograph.
Donald Trump's damage to the Republican Party, although already considerable, has barely begun... Republicans might have an even worse election in 2020.
keyStatements.context: Urging voters to vote against Republicans in 2018 midterms

keyStatements.quote: Trump is what he is, a floundering, inarticulate jumble of gnawing insecurities and not-at-all compensating vanities, which is pathetic.

background: George Will is a Pulitzer Prize-winning conservative columnist who has written for The Washington Post since 1974. A leading intellectual voice of conservatism for decades, he was a registered Republican for most of his adult life. However, he left the Republican Party shortly before the 2016 Republican National Convention due to Trump's nomination, switching his registration to 'unaffiliated.'
oppositionReasons: Believes Trump lacks the character, temperament, and intellect for presidency
Views Trump as antithetical to conservative principles and values
Opposes Trump's coarse rhetoric and attacks on institutions
Believes Trump has corrupted the Republican Party beyond recognition
notableActions: Left the Republican Party in 2016 over Trump's nomination
Urged voters to vote against Republicans in 2018 to punish GOP for enabling Trump
Wrote dozens of columns systematically dismantling Trump's policies and character
Consistently defended constitutional conservatism against Trumpism

strengths: Over 50 years of influential conservative writing
Deep knowledge of political philosophy and American history
Reputation for intellectual rigor and principled conservatism
Widely respected across political spectrum for consistency
considerations: Some view him as out of touch with working-class conservatives
His brand of conservatism has less influence in Trump-era GOP
Critics say he represents elitist establishment conservatism
Never held elected office or government position
